mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-rate environments are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal shows no predictive power, with the interaction term p-value exceeding 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 0.5 percent monthly signals strong supply-side momentum that translates to positive short-term S&P 500 returns during expansion phases.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power during recession or stagflation regimes, with direction accuracy dropping below 50 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with a rising federal funds rate indicates a tightening cycle that compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Returns remain positive in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, invalidating the tightening compression effect.

mechanism_narrative: Higher total nonfarm payrolls reflect robust economic activity and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, indicating no persistent predictive relationship.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent quarterly confirms sustained economic expansion, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict direction in more than 65 percent of cases when GDP crosses the 2.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening phase signals supply constraints and slowing output, leading to negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Positive returns dominate during tightening phases despite falling production, with accuracy below 45 percent.
